summary: web3-secrets-detector ships wallet/system scanner with hardcoded C2 exfiltration
details: Package presents itself as a Web3 secrets detector but ships three modules (build-verifier.js, scanner.js, wallet-scanner.js) that collect host identity (os.hostname(), username, cwd) and POST it to a hardcoded attacker-controlled endpoint at https://ddjidd564.github.io (a personal GitHub Pages account, not a publisher-aligned domain). wallet-scanner.js (~1300 lines) combines child_process, fs, https, curl invocations, and execSync to enumerate the host and reach out over the network — the wallet-stealer / system-info-exfiltration shape. scanner.js performs `whoami` / `os.hostname()` collection and POSTs the result to the same C2. build-verifier.js issues `docker push` via execSync alongside outbound https.request POSTs. The combination of hardcoded non-publisher C2, host/identity collection, wallet-targeted scanning, and shell command execution is a credential/wallet stealer with active exfiltration to an attacker-controlled destination.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
